title: tillandsia racinaea
intro: Tillandsia racinaea, also known as the 'Rocky Mountain Air Plant', is a type of epiphytic bromeliad that obtains moisture and nutrients from the air, rather than through roots. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's extracts have been found to have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects, which can help to boost the immune system and protect against oxidative stress. Tillandsia racinaea can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
